text: Hey! There you are, you drunken bastard. I did it, so pay up.
Whoa, not so fast there, friend. You owe me money.
You and Sam dared me to go into that bandit camp. Well I did and brought back his stupid hat like you said, so give me the 10,000 gold you promised.
We agreed to 750 gold. I want my money.
So you're telling me you and Sam just sent me in there to die, then? I want my money, you bastard.
Thanks. You and Sam drink too hard for me. Think I'll take my gold and go buy a nice bit of swamp in Black Marsh.
Thanks. You and Sam drink too hard for me. Think I'll take my gold and go buy something for this headache.
We caroused together with Sam Guevenne, remember? We broke into the Temple of Dibella? How much did you end up drinking?
Hey, where do you think you're going? Come back here.
Thank Her Benevolence it wasn't burned like the other tomes.
Well done. I wasn't certain you'd even know what to look for.
So endeth the lesson.
Back from whence you came, heathen!
To Oblivion with you.
Forgive me, my brother.
Mara forgives you, my child.
Rest well and sleep deeply, my brother.
Lady Mara... preserve me...
I've failed you... forgive me...
Too much... can't...
Lady Mara... I await your embrace...
No... not when we're so close...
Can't fail now... must hang on...
How dare you defile this temple, Orc!
Begone, invader!
Die, foul Orc!
You'll pay for your blasphemy!
Awaken, and perish!
Feel the Benevolence of Mara!
I'll send you to Oblivion!
This is your final hour!
We must end the nightmares!
For Lady Mara!
For Dawnstar!
By Her Benevolence, you shall not stop us!
Take heed! They've awakened!
Time for your final rest!
They mustn't stop us!
We fight till the last!
How could you, Erandur...
Vaermina forgive me...
Fine, I'll show you, you big bully.
Trust your instincts, Dovahkiin. Your blood will show you the way.
Not intentionally. Some hoped he would be gone forever, forever lost. Meyye.
I knew better. Tiid bo amativ. Time flows ever onward. One day he would surface.
Which is why I have lived here. For thousands of mortal years I have waited. I knew where he would emerge but not when.
Hmm. How to explain in your tongue? The dov have words for such things that joorre do not.
It is an... artifact from outside time. It does not exist, but it has always existed. Rah wahlaan. They are... hmm... fragments of creation.
The Kelle... Elder Scrolls, as you name them, they have often been used for prophecy. Yes, your prophecy comes from an Elder Scroll.
But this is only a small part of their power. Zofaas suleyk.
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as elf, but as dovah!
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Orc, but as dovah!
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Khajiit, but as dovah!
Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Argonian, but as dovah!
Just a pilgrim. I'd prefer to leave it at that, if you don't mind.
Walking the Steps. Meditating on the emblems. I make this trip every few years.
I'm walking the 7,000 Steps to honor the Greybeards and reflect on my own life. It's a tradition among the Nords of Skyrim.
I like to spend time up here. Walk the Steps, meditate on the emblems. Doesn't hurt when I bag some game along the way.
Enjoying the hospitalities of the inn. Suggest you do the same.
They're not the sort to take visitors, but I never go that high up the path anyway.
Some folk who make the trip leave them food or other essentials, but not to make conversation.
I was just outside Ivarstead when it happened. It's an exciting moment. Nothing like this has happened in centuries.
I did. Strange days when the monks will do that. I wonder what it means.
Keep an eye out for wolves if you're headed up the path to High Hrothgar.
All right. I wrote down the last place I'd heard about. Don't get killed.
There are more books to be recovered and secured in the Arcanaeum.
Looks like you're still in one piece. And more importantly, so is the book. Thank you.
Some other time, then.
I just try to keep out of the way when they start roughhousing.
Don't mind them. Just working out some issues, in the old way.
Little blood to clear the air. Hope you have the stomach for it, outsider.
What is it?
Not now, I'm trying to watch this!
Pay attention, you could learn something.
Are you going to let me watch, or not?
Look at the young ones scrap!
Twelve septims on the girl! Look at that speed.
Out of the way!
Could you move? You're in the way.
Hope they don't get carried away here.
Good fight going, here.
Is it that obvious? I should take pains to seem more happy. I wouldn't want to upset our host.
But the truth of it is, I have a mountain of paperwork back in Windhelm and it's not getting any smaller.
I just hope this party doesn't drag on all night.
Well enough, I suppose.
Elenwen puts a great deal of effort into these parties, and it shows.
I just wonder what her real motivations are. That woman is inscrutable.
I beg your pardon?
You must have me mistaken for someone else. I am Elisif, widow of High King Torygg and Jarl of Solitude.
Lucky for you, I possess a forgiving nature and I'm not easily insulted.
Ill luck. I'm a busy man and I don't have time for this sort of frivolity.
But at the same time, I don't dare refuse an invitation from Elenwen.
We have a relationship of mutual respect.
I care little about the politics and rivalries between the Thalmor and the Empire.
But I do respect power, and the Thalmor have that. They leave me alone, and I leave them alone.
By the gods, let this mine be small so we can be done with it quickly.
Very much so, yes.
Elenwen's parties are always so elegant. She really has impeccable taste.
My only regret is that she doesn't host them more often.
Elenwen is a formidable woman. She lacks for neither cunning nor ambition.
Now that I think of it, she would have made a fine Imperial general.
A delicate question, to be sure. I'm the Emperor's cousin, and the Empire has little love for the Thalmor.
On the other hand, the Thalmor have considerable funds and are looking to expand their shipping.
I haven't signed any deals yet, but I'm sure my cousin would want a cut if I do. What better way to soothe his wounded pride?
It is not eyes that reveal another's true nature. It is the heart.
If your eyes and your ears are open and your mind is free of judgement and expectation, you might learn much in this chamber.
There are words spoken, and words unspoken. Beware these Thalmor, for they are adept in both languages.
Now here is a face I did not expect to see. Be mindful, for you walk among adders.
That's putting it mildly.
The Thalmor invite me here to remind everyone that they can tell the Empire what to do.
I would refuse, but I don't want to jeopardize the peace between us.
I am not. I have no more taste for Thalmor wine than I do for Thalmor company.
What is more, I'm away from the hold at a time when there are dragons and Stormcloaks on the loose.
There's every chance I'll return to find a pile of smoking ash where Whiterun once stood.
I trust them completely, because I understand them.
When the Thalmor want something, they tell you straight. In my experience, they have no interest in subtleties and innuendo.
So if one of them says they're going to do something, I believe them. When you can just take what you want, there's no need to lie.
Yes, I know what you mean. Most Nords struggle to accept change.
Those who fought the Thalmor in the Great War still see an enemy. But that war is over, and a new day has dawned.
Besides, the Thalmor reward their friends richly. Anyone who doesn't take advantage of that is a stubborn fool.
Not well at all, and I'm happy to keep it that way.
That woman and her Thalmor Justiciars have caused nothing but problems for the people of Skyrim.
A jarl has many duties. Attending parties isn't the most important one, but neither is it unpleasant.
Take advantage of the fine food and drink. Such things have been scarce since the war began.
I came here hoping to learn that more Imperial reinforcements were on their way to Skyrim, but so far I've been disappointed.
I admit, I've never felt entirely at ease in the Thalmor Embassy.
There is peace now, and that peace will continue for as long as it suits our needs.
But make no mistake, this is not a peace forged out of necessity between rival nations of equal strength.
It is more like the calm between storms. And the next storm, I think, will be far deadlier than the last.
As much as can be expected, yes.
I have no complaints.
I suppose so, yes.
Everything is splendid, thank you.
Very much so, and thank you for asking.
Very much so, and thank you for asking.
I've spent most of my life as a trader. I've got a stake in several shipping ventures across Tamriel, mostly imports and exports.
I made a lot of friends in a lot of ports, and the Thalmor approach me with a generous offer.
I give them access to my business associates, and they keep my coffers full.
With any luck, a worthy hero will step forward and solve our problems, just like in the tales of old.
We must keep faith in ourselves and the Divines.
These are troubling times, indeed.
These are troubling times, indeed.
There are words spoken, and words unspoken. Beware these Thalmor, for they are adept in both languages.
For this reason, I avoid them when I can.
When I married Torygg, he promised we'd go to lavish parties just like this. I wish he were here now.
Isn't this something? Elenwen really knows how to host a party!
I could learn a lot from the people in this room.
Have you tried the wine? It's better than anything in the Blue Palace.
We've got our hands full dealing with the Stormcloaks. What about you?
Are the dragons frightening your people as much as they're frightening mine?
Have you found it difficult to get provisions lately? I can scarcely feed the staff in my palace.
Between the dragons and the Stormcloaks, my coffers are running dry.
I've known her long enough to appreciate her excellent taste in food and wine.
And also long enough to respect her importance to Skyrim. She plays a vital role in rooting out the forbidden worship of Talos.
As far as I'm concerned, those Talos worshippers get what they deserve. All they do is cause problems for me and the other jarls.
It's true. For as long as I can remember our crops have always done well.
I'm not sure whether it's the soil, the climate or the favor of the Divines, but we've never had a poor harvest.
Quite often, yes. The only thing I enjoy more than luxury is sharing that luxury with those who can appreciate it.
But I also enjoy learning the latest gossip and hearing the most intriguing rumors.
Oh, hello there. I didn't expect to see you here.
Yes, I am indeed. Elenwen always serves the best wine at her parties. The food is excellent as well.
Though I confess, being surrounded by so many wealthy and important people makes me feel more than a little nervous.
I lead the Justiciars. We are charged with enforcing the ban on Talos worship.
Nords can be quite stubborn. They're slow to change their ways, and we find more heretics every day.
But mark me well -- I will purge the heresy from this land as surely as the flame burns the flesh from the bone.
Of course not. One does not gather the most important men and women of Skyrim and then serve them cheap ale and stale bread.
This is only a taste of the benefits that accrue to those that align themselves with Thalmor interests.
We've had the same troubles.
I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
Fair faces and fine food are well and good, but honesty is rarely so pleasing.
I am the Steward of Whiterun. I serve Jarl Balgruuf in Dragonsreach.
Truth be told, I'd be more comfortable if I were back there. I get nervous in the company of so many worthies.
These Thalmor know how to treat a guest.
A ruler is entitled to luxury, don't you think? The Thalmor understand that.
Why would anyone join the Stormcloaks? They have a fraction of the Empire's wealth and power.
If I had my way, I'd spend all my time at parties like this, and none of it dealing with the common rabble.
Have you learned nothing about me? I have dealings with everyone.
Now, leave me. I don't want to arouse suspicion by talking to someone that clearly doesn't belong here.
Are you enjoying the party?
Is the food and drink to your liking?
Is my staff seeing to your needs?
This gathering is nothing more than a boast. The Thalmor are reminding us that we're at their beck and call.
We've got troubles enough without the Thalmor and their witch-hunts.
I'd rather be back at Whiterun, but while I'm here I might as well get some real news from the other parts of Skyrim.
These Thalmor might not like to admit it, but as long as the Empire has Skyrim, the Empire has strength.
Born and raised right here in Rorikstead.
My father was a soldier. He fought in the great war and when it was over, he retired here to raise a family.
My mother passed away when I was just a babe, so he did his best to raise me on his own.
It's not the most exciting place in the world, but the people here work hard and don't cause trouble for anyone.
Not every Nord is obsessed with strength, honor and skill at arms.
A rare few are born with a gift for making money. My investments are my strength and my wealth is my weapon.
As for honor, well, there are some luxuries even I can't afford.
Oh, yes! Quite! So many important people from all across Skyrim.
Splendid. Call if you have need of me.
It pleases me to hear you say so.
Excellent. I'll leave you to it, then.
Don't mention the Guild. In fact, it's better if you don't speak to me at all. This is not the place.
For now, perhaps. But I've heard disturbing rumors that they're marshaling their strength.
I suspect the treaty that ended the war was just a tactic to buy time so the Thalmor can rebuild their armies.
I hope I'm wrong. I'm not sure the Empire can afford another war.
Why, thank you. You are most courteous.
I admit, I'm surprised to see you here. Normally these parties are reserved for the jarls and business leaders of Skyrim.
Elenwen must be very fond of you.
Yarr!
Horkers' revenge!
This is for Haldyn!
Wait, where did you get this? This piece was thought to be lost forever, please take this with my utmost thanks!
Careful, huh? Thanks, Skulvar. I'll remember that. Ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
I see no harm in it, if you have the skill.
The grindstone will improve your weapons. Use the table to improve your armor.
If you've got the raw materials, you can use the forge to make something new.
I guess you don't have potatoes in your ears after all. Just make sure we get a fresh batch in soon.
That's a fair price. My blade is yours.
Looks like you've got some help already. I'll join you, but you'll have to let your comrade go. What do you say?
I'm what you'd call of soldier of fortune. Make me an offer, and I just might fight at your side.
If you've got the gold to pay my fee, then my sword arm is yours.
Can't be too careful these days, with the war and other dangers. For a fair price, I'll watch your back.
Some call me a mercenary, but I like to think of myself as a freelance adventurer for hire.
My skill in battle is unmatched. Fortunately for you, that skill can be bought.
By blade or by spell, if you want it dead, I'll make it dead. All you need to do is pay my fee.
Blade and shadow, silence and death - these are my arts. For a modest fee, I'll make great art for you.
If it's a mercenary you're looking for, you've found him. There's no stronger sword arm for hire in all of Skyrim.
Truly? So maybe my mother isn't crazy after all.
You had best tell the Jarl in Whiterun. He needs to know about this.
Yep. Ale's going bad.
Did you hear me?
The ale is going bad. We need to get a new batch.
Hard not to.
Orgnar.
Orgnar!
Are you listening?
Lucan's got a little bit of everything at the Riverwood Trader.
If you need weapons or armor, see Alvor, the blacksmith.
Don't see that every day.
I've never seen anything quite like that.
Mighty impressive.
Wow! That's impressive.
By the Hist, what a sight...
Impressive. Damn impressive.
Wow. Would you look at that?
By Ysmir! That's impressive.
Gotta admit, that's impressive.
By the Nine!
He would dote on her so much. Let her work the forge and everything. I miss him so.
Riverwood's agreeable enough, I suppose. For a Nord village.
I understand. Speaking to a goddess is overwhelming for many. Return when you're prepared.
Oh, a customer. Sorry you had to hear that.
I'm with the Vigil of Stendarr. We believe this house might have been used for daedra worship. Evil rites and so forth.
Coward. You're nothing. Nothing!
I can't talk about it. Rest assured, the jarl hears everyone's concerns. You will be able to visit the dead again soon.
I don't need to know the details. Blessings of Arkay to you for your help.
Here. Take my amulet as a reward.
This shrine was built by the Dunmer. As our land was scorched by fire and brimstone, Azura's prophecies led us to safety.
She is a daedra, a powerful being who watches from beyond our mortal plane. She has chosen you to be her champion.
Fine. I can't force you to help me, but the offer is open.
Is it dead?
Dovahkiin? No!!
You... must be Dragonborn!
Keep firing!
That's it! We'll bring that bastard down!
Is that all you've got?
For Whiterun and Skyrim!
Come down here and fight, you coward!
I can't get a bead on him!
Does anyone see him?
We don't stand a chance!
Stay under cover!
Now you're going to pay!
Hold it there! Keep your distance.
Take him down! It's our best chance!
I can't believe we actually killed a dragon!
Dragonborn... it's an honor.
You saved Whiterun today. We won't forget.
Is it really dead?
Come on, I want a better look at that thing.
I'm glad you're on our side.
Good luck. I hope you get there in time.
And what's wrong with him, huh?
I don't know where we're going, but Sovngarde awaits.
Ulfric? The Jarl of Windhelm? You're the leader of the rebellion.
But if they've captured you... Oh gods, where are they taking us?
Watch your tongue. You're speaking to Ulfric Stormcloak, the true high king.
Fascinating! So the ancients were more daring... or foolhardy... similar to the Dragon Break itself, although less far-reaching...
This Elder Scroll might be able to show you the past in some way, then... yes, yes, it could work.
Return it here, to the Tiid-Ahraan. Then... Kelle vomindok. Nothing is certain with such things...
But I believe the Scroll's bond with the Tiid-Ahraan will allow you a... a seeing, a vision of the moment of its creation.
Then you will feel - know - Dragonrend, in the power of its first expression.
You will see them... wuth fadonne... my friends - Hakon, Gormlaith, Felldir.
Do you know the College of Winterhold? They have a deep interest in such arcana.
Hmm. Well, if that's what the Greybeards told you, I confess I don't have a better plan.
So little is known about the Elder Scrolls, almost anything is possible where they're concerned. Artifacts of truly inconceivable power.
Paarthurnax? That name is familiar... the legendary leader of the Greybeards?
I would not have expected a scholar of such an esoteric turn of mind among their ranks.
What are you waiting for?
Just keep swinging!
Pull him back!
Aye.
Hey, a fight!
Vilkas, take him out to the yard and see what he can do.
Vilkas, take her out to the yard and see what she can do.
Come on now, watch the footwork. Keep your balance.
Strike when the shoulder turns. He's giving you openings!
Watch the eyes!
Just keep moving, you'll wear him out!
Are those two at it again?
Nice!
Come on!
I've been working on a shield for Aela.
My wife is in mourning and I need to get back to her soon. I'd be much obliged if you could take this to Aela for me.
Wait, before you go...
Great, back to the fight.
Always good to see 'em go.
So who's winning now?
Keep a sharp eye about you and maybe I'll teach you to fight some day.
Watch the blades.
Vilkas will see if you're worth our trouble.
I hear you have spirit. This is good. You may yet join our numbers.
Keep up the effort, boy. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Keep up the effort, young one. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Get him!
Chop chop!
Get in there and push!
All right!
I won't be the one to go back there. I won't return to Avanchnzel. You can't make me do it.
Then you didn't know Roggvir, and you have no idea what it means to be a Nord of Skyrim!
He did the honorable thing, even when he knew it would cost him everything.
My brother was the best man in Solitude.
Depends on what you mean by "busy." I'm also the head jailor in the dungeon here. Quiet job, usually.
Roggvir. He opened a gate and they executed him for it.
He opened a gate and the wrong man rode out of it. If that man hadn't been Ulfric Stormcloak. If Ulfric hadn't killed High King Torygg...
But it was Ulfric. And he did kill the high king in honorable combat. My brother refused to allow the Imperials to take revenge for the deed.
So now he's dead.
You mean Roggvir. Yes. I made an appeal to Captain Aldis. He allowed Roggvir to have a proper Nord burial, as is the custom in Solitude.
Work in it? I own it.
You could say that. I own the place.
You must bring it to Avanchnzel, in the west. Return it to them. It must go back.
Then like Roggvir, you're a Nord at heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand.
I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had...
I... I think I need to stop talking now...
This city has a long history of madness and murder. The Wolf Queen... Pelagius... The death of High King Torygg... And now, public executions.
My books tell the stories. Have no doubt - as pretty as its streets are, as jovial as the bards may be... darkness is drawn to Solitude.
Listen, Roggvir and I grew up together. It was us, a few others and Vivienne. Vivienne Onis.
I have no idea why, but one day, I told Roggvir I liked her. It was a stupid thing to do... He mocked me incessantly after that.
From that point forward, every time Vivienne and I were anywhere close to each other, he made sure to humiliate me.
He'd beat me up, dump mead on my head, anything he could get away with. Once he even slipped a beehive into my bed.
We were children, sure. But Roggvir never outgrew that immaturity, or his cruelty. So any talk about him being an "honorable" Nord? Pure folly.
Sometimes you just do what needs to be done. You don't have to hate a man to kill him.
Though, admittedly... it helps.
Oh, if you're looking for a good attitude go see the bards. They'll fake it for you if nothing else.
It is done. I won't go back to Avanchnzel. I won't.
I could have left it behind. I could have put it back where it came from.
Oh, wow. I knew it. I knew they were real! I have to remember to tell Papa.
No. I think he was entirely wrong. Roggvir's execution was justified and necessary.
Being honorable might make you a good man, but it doesn't make you right. Be a better world if it did.
Let's just say that the headsman's axe may not be the worst way for a Solitude jail prisoner to die.
Look at that. I've told you to watch your tongue and mine is waggling. I've said enough.
I showed up to watch, yeah.
I don't know if they were right to execute him for what he did, but I'll tell you something...
It couldn't have happened to a nicer guy.
Really? Was it big? Did it breathe fire?
Not my finest hour. Roggvir was a fine and honorable man.
No. That honor is Sybille Stentor's. A smart man steers clear of Stentor.
And keeps himself out of the dungeon when she's having a bad day...
Dragon?
You really don't know?
He lets Ulfric Stormcloak escape Solitude. Ulfric rides in, murders the high king, and Roggvir lets him ride right back out.
Then we should soon be selling arrows by the bushel. A sword is no good against a dragon.
Where the dragon attacked?
We've heard rumors of death and... and fire from the skies. But no real news.
Can any man hope to be ready for a dragon?
I can but hone their skills and harden their hearts. The rest is up to the gods.
You're the Dragonborn, aren't you?
I'm sort of hoping you are going to fight the dragons for us. A normal man is no match for a dragon.
Don't forget me next time you need herbs and potions.
Then like Roggvir, you have a Nord's heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand.
I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had...
I... I think I need to be done talking for the moment.
I'm not looking for conversation.
I'm in mourning. Can't you see that?
New in town, right? If you need a place to sleep, head over to the Winking Skeever. We keep a fine inn.
You know, the Winking Skeever's not just the best inn in Solitude. It's the also the only inn in Solitude.
Ain't got one. But I'd fancy a chance at that Jaree-Ra's scrawny neck.
Filthy Argonian never would have set foot in this city, if High King Torygg was still alive. I can tell you that.
Oh, I am well. I will be well. Well to be within the will inside the walls.
Excellent. You've done us a great service. Here is your reward.
The jarl has put out a bounty to slay a giant. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
If you change your mind, seek me out here.
Oh, thank the gods. Here, take this with my thanks.
Here, I'll show you on your map so you can stay away. Please, if you can help me, let me know or get out of my way so I can try to find a priest.
You there, please you have to help me! I was attacked by a vampire! Do you have a potion or something that can cure this before I turn?
I'm on my way to Windhelm to join up with the Stormcloaks. Ulfric has the right of it.
The Empire is in shambles. They've banned the worship of Talos.
I may have roots in Cyrodiil, but Skyrim's as much my home as any Nord's.
I'm on my way to Solitude to join the Legion. A united Empire is better for everyone.
I was born and raised in Skyrim, but the Nords treat us like dirt.
I want to be on the right side.
Then get out of my way. I have to get to a town and find a priest.
The jarl has put out a bounty on Forsworn. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Really? Power? Haven't you already killed enough of your foes using the more mundane means at your disposal? Swords, magic, etc.? How... uninspired.
Defense? No one would dare attack Riften. I'm not sure you realize just how well connected Jarl Maven is with the Empire.
With a single snap of her fingers, we could have a legion here in a fortnight.
As steward, I serve as an intermediary between the jarl and her subjects.
I also handle the less important and day-to-day policies that affect daily life in Riften.
Lately I've had my hands full suppressing these ridiculous accusations of corruption in the keep.
I'm not certain what business it is of yours, but I'm Moth- I mean Jarl Maven's steward.
I run the prisons in Riften, keep the riff-raff from robbing the good folk of this city and have the drunks tossed into the lake.
As long as she continues to keep me at her side, she can count on my blade to protect her.
To be honest, I doubt someone would be stupid enough to make an attempt on Maven... sorry, Jarl Maven's life.
But just in case, I'll be ready for them.
Ulfric's a bloody hero. I don't think there's a worthy Nord alive who'd dare argue the point.
He's taking the Empire to task and making them answer for their crimes.
I don't think I'll be content until Ulfric sits upon the throne of Skyrim with the ashes of the Concordat at his feet.
Hey, if the Empire and the Stormcloaks want to kill each other over some foolish cause, I say let 'em.
All I care about is two things: watchin' Maven's back and countin' the gold she pays me to do it.
I don't care what you think. The Empire put Maven on the throne for a reason. That's all you need to know.
If you say so.
Indeed. You don't get to the position my mother's achieved by blind luck... her business sense and diplomatic skills are legendary.
The throne suits me, don't you think?
My title is just a formality. I've always been in charge around here.
Jarl Maven Black-Briar. Has a pleasant sound to it, wouldn't you say?
Speak. I wish to hear everything my subjects have to say.
Unmid and Anuriel shouldn't blame themselves for Riften's fall. I am the one who's ultimately responsible.
If Riften can be retaken, I will redouble my efforts to ensure the safety of my subjects and the defense of The Rift.
I presume you have something important to say?
If you're here to report any instances of corruption, be confident that we're handling these rumors accordingly.
The Imperials now control Riften and have placed Maven Black-Briar on the jarl's throne.
Perhaps the Stormcloaks might rally to take back Riften one day. Only time will tell.
There's nothing more I can teach you.
You've already learned all I can teach you.
You already know all that I know.
It's as rewarding as it is dangerous. Let's see what I can show you.
With a master of magic at your side, you'll have nothing to fear.
The only thing better than a powerful mage fighting at your side is... well, nothing, really.
Why hire a common soldier to protect you when you can have a master of the arcane?
For a modest fee, I'll bring my formidable arcane powers to bear against your foes. What do you say?
Why settle for just stabbing your foes when you can roast them alive in a gout of arcane fire?
Watch your back, friend. Better yet, pay me to do it for you.
My blade is sharp and I thirst for battle. Onward, then!
Best not to walk the roads of Skyrim alone.
Sellswords are common in Skyrim, but you'll find precious few as stout and as strong as I am.
If you're looking to hire the strongest mercenary in Skyrim, I'd wager your search is over.
I've fought in more battles than I've got years of life in me. Pay my fee and I'll put that experience to work for you, friend.
Death is my art, and like all artists, I seek a patron. For a mere handful of gold, I will follow you into any danger.
I know nothing of fear, and nothing of remorse. Pay my fee, and together we will vanquish any foe.
I am a lethal instrument, yours to command for a modest sum. I suggest you hire me before one of your enemies does.
We welcome you to worship the Eight Divines, freely and openly.
I'm afraid I'm running errands at the moment. But please, visit me later at the Temple of the Divines.
Feel free to take a seat, or pray at the shrines. We appreciate worship in all its forms.
Come by the Winking Skeever any time you want to hear a tune.
You should think about staying the night. Corpulus keeps a clean inn.
Come back if you want to play tag.
Papa doesn't like when I talk to customers, anyway.
Remember the Winking Skeever next time you're footsore.
Need anything, you just say the word.
I was done talking anyway.
Yeah. Next time.
May song speed you along.
May the words of poets keep you warm.
May all your adventures be worthy of telling.
Now you can say you've met the most talented bard in all of Skyrim.
Don't forget my name. Someday, everyone will be saying it.
Glad that distraction's over...
We're done then? Good.
I suppose I should return to my papers...
Always good to talk to a fellow bard.
Farewell, stranger.
Stop by again, sometime. I'll sing for you.
Anytime, friend.
Always time for a fellow bard.
Oh, if you haven't heard Dean Pantea sing, you should.
We should talk again sometime.
Always glad to talk to a bard.
Thanks for taking the time. People never want to talk to me.
Time to finally see the sky, and make it rain red.
Okay, I'll go now.
I'm glad I could help.
And you've proven that was the best move I could have made. Don't give me that look. You're free to go.
Here, how about a little token for your efforts? My family's ring and all the things the guards confiscated from you when you were jailed.
Now if you'll excuse me, I have to figure out how we're going to fill our recently emptied mine.
Anything I can do.
Always glad to talk to the bard who returned part of the Poetic Edda.
It's been an honor.
I'll get back to court business, then.
Always have your shield at the ready.
Keep your sword arm strong.
Take care of your armor, and it'll take care of you.
I must return to the court. They simply can't make do without me.
Good, we've wasted enough time here.
We'll speak again later. If we must...
I'll be sure to check "Talk to random stranger" off my list of today's tasks...
You are free to leave.
You are excused.
I am done with you. You may go.
Be well. Pray for Solitude and my husband.
Keep the faith. General Tullius will win this war.
We have the utmost confidence in you and Ulfric's guiding hands.
May the blessings of the Eight be with you.
May the blessings of the Nine be with you.
Feel free to return to me with questions. The Blue Palace is an open forum.
Please feel welcome in the court of Solitude.
I won't forget what you've done for Solitude.
Anyone with a stout heart like yours is welcome here.
I'm proud to call a man like you friend.
I'm proud to call a woman like you friend.
Do not dishonor or attempt to harm Elisif in any way. Adhere to that rule, and you'll be fine.
I must return my attention to Elisif. If I were to lose another jarl while I was... distracted...
Take care of yourself, and always remember: the world is ripe with people looking to spill your blood...
Stay guarded. You never know what could be out there.
All right. Back to slicing, dicing, and baking.
Back to my errands.
Darkness guide you.
Yeah, yeah. Goodbye, kill lots of people, Hail Sithis, and all that.
Have a good contract.
Kill well, and often.
Just come back alive, and I'll be happy.
Go on now. Time to get your hands bloody.
Take care of yourself, beef roast.
Later, ham hock.
Yeah, good luck.
Represent us well, brother.
Represent us well, sister.
Remember, when you kill - we all wield the blade.
Watch yourself out there.
Cicero bids you... farewell.
Oh, yes. Goodbye, goodbye.
Farewell, farewell!
Skuli owns the inn with his mother gone, but I handle the rooms.
You should head on inside. Eydis or Skuli can take care of you.
Are you finished ogling the grotesque? I suppose I should be grateful you didn't simply attack me.
We are traveling to the Shrine of Kynareth in Whiterun. I hear the Gildergreen is more beautiful than ever since it's been restored.
They say that eating the fruit it bears will add years to your life.
And I have no more time for talking. Go speak with Kesh if you're so curious.
Returning to High Rock. Our shepherd lost his way, and I fear Peryite's wrath may consume those who remain with him.
Kesh could tell you more. I just want out of Skyrim as quickly as possible.
I am one of the Afflicted. I'd have been dead from this plague a year ago if not for Peryite's protection.
Don't mess anything up while you're here...
Good talking to you. Not many care to chat with the help.
Okay. I need to get back to the palace soon, anyway.
You can catch me at the Blue Palace later, if you want to talk again.
Uh, I guess I should be getting back to work. It was more fun talking, though.
May Arkay bless you with a long life.
May the light of truth keep the darkness at bay.
It's best you leave me be.
Sure, you make your retreat and I'll make mine.
Keep your head about you.
Just watch yourself. You don't want to meet me on the job.
Tell these horkers to set me free.
Leave me be. I'm no harm here.
Keep my men in your prayers. They'll need them if they don't shape up.
Stay strong. The Eight willing, we'll win this war.
Sure, whatever you say. Bye.
Always good talking to you... friend.
Thanks for talking to me.
For a stranger, you're not so bad.
Blessed are those who worship, for they know true grace.
Keep the faith.
The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Eight.
The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Nine.
Your rewards in life will match the strength of your faith.
The Temple of Divines is always open for worshippers.
Take care, and go with the blessings of the Eight.
Take care, and go with the blessings of the Nine.
I really need to get back to planning my wedding.
Now if you'll excuse me. The East Empire Company has several accounts, and it's up to me to balance them.
Perhaps I'll be seeing more of you.
Please come back if you're ever in need of fletching supplies.
Good to talk to a fellow traveler.
Stop back anytime.
Thank you for helping my aunt earlier. I'll see you around.
People to see and places to go, huh? Later, then.
I understand, you're an important person. I'll catch up with you later.
I should make sure Svari is okay.
Addvar won't sell anything if I don't go and check up on him.
Come back to Bits and Pieces anytime.
Make sure to recommend us to your associates.
Return anytime you need arms or armor. My forge never rests.
Keep safe. And always wear your armor.
Yes, I agree with you. Have you heard any news from the other parts of Skyrim?
You speak truly. Have you heard any interesting rumors of late?
I'll not argue that. Heard any interesting gossip lately?
I couldn't agree more. By the way, have you heard any interesting rumors lately?
I couldn't have said it better myself. It's been some time since I've had news from elsewhere in Skyrim. What have you heard?
I know just what you mean. Heard anything interesting lately? Any news or gossip?
I heard Elenwen say she's going open a bottle of her best Firebrand Wine later. I can hardly wait.
These Stormcloaks are nothing more than an armed mob. This war won't last much longer.
I hope they'll serve the roast venison with bittergreen sauce that we had at the last party.
That was delicious.
This war has taken a toll on the merchants of Skyrim. Let's hope that it ends soon, for their sake.
I look forward to the day when this rebellion is put down, and we once again have peace.
The Gray Quarter in Windhelm is little better than a slum. It's shameful that the Dark Elves are made to live that way.
With the soldiers all fighting in the war, there's nobody to hunt down the bandits. Travel has become dangerous of late.
It's been a brutal winter. I can't remember the last time it was this cold, and for this long.
Do enjoy yourself.
I need another drink.
Goodbye, then.
Farewell.
Don't let me keep you.
Until later, then.
May wisdom light your path.
Good speaking with you.
Until next time.
Keep an eye on these Thalmor. You never know...
Come and visit me in Solitude sometime.
Very well then.
It was a pleasure, thank you.
Well, I did hear that the Argonians have been banned from living inside the walls of Windhelm.
They're only allowed on the city docks now. Most of them sleep together in one building, and they're as poor as wretches.
Have you visited Rorikstead lately? No matter how harsh the winter, the crops that grow there never falter.
Even when every other farm in the hold is barely scraping by, the harvest at Rorikstead is always bountiful.
Is it good fortune? The blessings of the Divines? Who knows?
Now that I think on it, I did hear something rather interesting the other day.
Apparently, the Red Wave has been docked in Solitude for well over a month, and with no sign of shipping out any time soon.
Some say the ship suffered damage to her hull when she struck an iceberg, and Captain Safia can't afford the repairs.
Of course, she would never admit such a thing. Captain Safia is far too proud a woman for that.
Well, there might be nothing to it, but I've heard that the feud between the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns in Whiterun has grown ugly.
Fralia Gray-Mane is convinced that the Battle-Borns have abducted one of her sons.
I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane.
It's not really a fair comparison, if you ask me.
After all, Eorlund works the Skyforge. How is any smith going to compete with that?
Well, I did see something interesting not long ago.
There were several Khajiit camped outside the walls of Whiterun. Seems they've been traveling around the whole of Skyrim peddling their wares.
You've got to admire their courage, doing something like that with dragons on the loose and a war going on.
Make yourself useful and chop up some firewood. There's a few coins in it for you.
Honest gold for honest work.
I heard those mages up in Winterhold have been trying to figure out how many dragons there are in Skyrim.
They seem to think it's at least a dozen, but I'm sure they're exaggerating.
They're probably trying to scare us into giving them more money and influence, so they can save us from the dragons threat.
Well, I did hear that Ulfric Stormcloak was slain while defending Windhelm against a dragon, but it sounds too good to be true.
I'll wager it was Ulfric himself that started the rumor. Maybe he thought it would bolster recruitment.
How much worse is this going to get?
We need to get in there and stop this!
I'm as ready as I'm going to be.
Not until we're all ready. We have to go together.
What are we waiting for?
She... she didn't make it.
When it was clear we were going to have to fall back, she stayed behind and made sure the rest of us were all right.
Hold up. There's a bear just ahead. See her?
I guess we're lucky that didn't come down on top of us.
Whew. That was close.
I'd rather not tangle with her right now. We might be able to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
Let's see where this goes.
We'd better push on. I'm sure the others will find another way out.
Damn it. No going back that way.
Easy does it now.
Ah, indeed. Well, there's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. I'm sure you'll manage, though.
Su'um ahrk morah.
Lok, Thu'um.
The Word calls you. Go to it.
Why do you intrude here, if not for tinvaak?
I have spoken. The Rotmulaag waits.
Look for yourself.
Ancano's power is growing. We can't crack whatever magic he's using to shield himself.
I hope your trip to Labyrinthian was worth it.
Bless you, friend. You've renewed my hope.
Before I came into Zenithar's calling, I confess I was a thief. But I learned a few things in my time that might help you survive. Let me show you.
Windhelm has been hard on my people. But our fortunes will turn, and we have shelter and food.
We have not fared well outside our native Black Marsh, but we're determined to make the best of things.
I began praying to Zenithar, the god of wealth, to bring us some fortune, but thieves made off with my sacred amulet almost as soon as I bought it.
Shahvee would be in your debt, kind one.
Not your concern, anyway.
Food, experience and a roof over my head in the finest city in Skyrim.
If money's all you hold dear, then you end up like Jaree-Ra. A plague on the city.
I was a member of the court during the reign of Torygg's father. It was Torygg who appointed me to the position.
Ha! I like you.
No man is a match for a dragon.
Buying one of our blades wouldn't hurt your chances, though.
Why of course. And will you also be needing the bow that shoots rainbows? Or perhaps a quiver that dispenses beer?
Look, if you want arrows we have them, but don't insult my intelligence.
Captain Aldis can't prove it, but that Argonian is up to no good.
Really? I knew he was mixed up in something dangerous, but I hadn't heard he was dead.
Finally...
Thank the gods for the end of yet another pointless conversation...
Thank you for your patronage.
I do hope you'll remain in Solitude. This city could do with some new blood.
Remember Radiant Raiment when you need a change of attire.
May your sword feast on Stormcloak flesh.
Good hunting. Go kill some bandits for me.
If you're off to fight, talk to Beirand before you go. His armor is well forged and dependable.
These are troubled times. Be on your guard out there.
May the gods speed your journeys... and steady your sword arm.
Come back when you figure out how to spend money.
I'll always have fruit to buy. Until it goes rotten. Then... I won't.
Good luck out there. It seems like a tough time to be wandering Skyrim.
Stay warm and keep sober. It's the two things I know for sure will make life better.
If you're heading up to Solitude you should stop to check out the view on your way. It's a good one.
Carriages to all the cities in Skyrim. Don't forget us.
A man like you should own a horse. Go speak with my husband.
Plenty of chores to do...
Pa always says good men need few words.
I want to check on Grani, one of our horses. Goodbye, stranger.
I'm looking for a place called Bard's Leap. They say the echo of a song sung from there can be heard all the way in Solitude.
Some may find their inspiration tucked away in tomes, or by carousing in the cities, but I find it here in the vast expanses of Skyrim.
The best tales are those of adventure. Who could truly write such a tale without first experiencing such?
Why to perform for my common man, of course! Why should only the courts and inns of wealthy towns be given the gift of music?
Observing and capturing the epic struggle for Skyrim! When brothers clash with bitter blades, surely there is a song to be found.
Grandson. He's a strong lad. His parents were at Helgen when, well you know.
Well, can't say I was a great father the first time around. Now that he's here it's like a second chance. He's a good lad.
It's okay, I know what happened at home. With the dragon and all.
I don't believe it. I made that bow myself. Been through some messes with it, let me tell you.
You know what? I want you to keep it. My great hunts are behind me, but yours are yet to come.
Check your map.
Yeah, but don't say anything about it to Froki. I don't think he understands.
It ain't so bad. I miss living with my parents back in Helgen, though. Froki can't cook and there's nobody to talk to up here.
Just what we need. City folk barging in, probably looking for an offering to their precious Divines.
I'm eleven years old, and I could take you.
Froki won't let me hunt on my own yet, but he never shuts up about killing his first stag when he was eleven.
Someday I'm going to kill a dragon all by myself.
You're Dragonborn. The rules don't apply to you. You can Shout the way dragons do... without training, through inborn instinct.
They always hope to teach the Dragonborn to respect the Way of the Voice as they do. They never fully succeed.
You'll have to make your own decision. It's a beautiful philosophy, but outside the seclusion of High Hrothgar, I was never able to hold to it.
Yes. Although I rarely use my training. The Greybeards believe the Voice should be used only for worship of Kynareth.
I have... fallen from their strict teaching, but I still don't feel it should be used lightly. Not all of Arngeir's lecturing was wasted, it seems.
Yes. The oldest and most powerful, although he may not seem so.
I doubt he's forgiven me for leaving. And for... well, for what he'd consider blasphemy. Using Shouts for anything but worship of Kynareth.
Yes. They chose me when I was just a lad. It was a great honor, of course. I was to become a Greybeard myself.
I spent almost ten years at High Hrothgar, learning the Way of the Voice. Then the Great War came... I couldn't stand missing it.
I often think about High Hrothgar. It's very... disconnected from the troubles down here. But that's why I couldn't stay, and why I couldn't go back.
I suppose the Greybeards care about Skyrim's troubles, in their way, but I needed to do something about it.
I'm sure Arngeir would call it one of my failings.
Yes, my lord.
No matter. Go to High Hrothgar. Learn what the Greybeards can teach you.
I know more than most. I trained with the Greybeards at High Hrothgar when I was a boy.
The Way of the Voice is an ancient, spiritual form of magic in which you project your vital essence into a "thu'um" or Shout.
Most people would need to train for years before they could even attempt a single Shout.
The Dragonborn is different - you have an inborn ability to Shout, the same way dragons are said to.
Back to business, Proventus. We still have a city to defend.
I envy you, you know. To climb the 7,000 Steps again... I made the pilgrimage once, did you know that?
High Hrothgar is a very peaceful place. Very... disconnected from the troubles of this world.
I wonder that the Greybeards even notice what's going on down here. They haven't seemed to care before.
It means you have the dragon blood - you have an inborn ability to use your Voice the way dragons do.
The old tales tell of Dragonborn heroes who slew dragons and took their power.
It seems those tales are true.
With the dragons returning, and now a Dragonborn appearing... maybe the Greybeards know what it all means.
It means you can kill dragons and steal their power - even speak with a dragon's voice, they say.
There hasn't been a Dragonborn seen in the land for centuries. Not since Talos himself walked the 7,000 Steps to High Hrothgar.
But remember, you're still oathbound to our cause. Skyrim still waits to be free.
They live in seclusion near the top of the Throat of the World, the great mountain of Skyrim.
They're masters of the Way of the Voice - of Shouting.
It's a great honor for them to summon you. They speak to very few - in fact, they hardly speak at all.
Ulfric can tell you more than most anyone. He was studying to be a Greybeard himself, back before the Great War.
My dear vagrant, we're the only family worth knowing in Markarth. We own everything in this city, even the jail.
And guess who owns the Silver-Blood Inn? Not that we named it that, the people just insisted.
You might make a decent Companion yet.
I hope the hunting goes well, brother. We've got the cowards on their heels now.
I hope the hunting goes well, sister. We've got the cowards on their heels now.
Have you been bringing the battle to the Silver Hand, friend? I wish I could be there with you. Soon enough.
Do you have guidance for me, Harbinger?
Vilkas said you're the new Kodlak. I don't know what that means, but you seem honorable, so that's good.
I think by now I've killed one of every living thing in Skyrim. May be time for a trip to Morrowind.
I never thought they would actually let me join the Companions. But now it looks like they're letting anyone in these days.
What guidance could you offer me?
Good to have some fresh eyes up there at the top. I follow your lead, Harbinger.
Oh, so you're the one in charge now? Fine, fine.
Just shut up and play the game.
I'm just saying that these ceramic lamps make me nervous. They just seem so... fragile.
I hope you've readied yourself.
Ah, there you are.
Hey, who's there?
Unless you've come to pay a ransom, turn around and walk away.
Oh, it's you. I didn't think you could handle this on your own. Did you get rid of Christer?
You cannot eat Gleda! Gleda is protected by Grok!
I sent a blessing to Mundus, a wasting plague that infected a scattering of Breton villages.
One of my monks, the elf Orchendor, was sent to gather these Afflicted. He shepherded them into Bthardamz for me, but has since lost his way.
I will not stand for betrayal. I want you to go to Bthardamz and kill Orchendor, in my name.
A joke? Alain Dufont... a joke? I am a king on my throne, and you, my friend... you will die slowly, and painfully.
Muiri? Muiri? So, what, you're the cow's champion? Oh, now that is rich. Well then, champion, let's get this over with.
I didn't know it when we were... with each other, but Alain is actually the leader of a band of cutthroats. Bandits.
They're holed up in some old dwarven ruin - Raldbthar. It's near Windhelm. They use it as their base. It's where they stage their raids.
I want you to go to that ruin, find Alain Dufont, and kill him. I don't care about his friends. Do whatever you want with them. But Alain has to die!
Ha! My opponent is so paralyzed with terror, no words will come. Let's be done with it, then!
How can we? We're a community of farmers, not fighting men.
If not for the war, we might have enough guards in the hold to protect the settlements. That the dragons should return now is most unfortunate.
These secret visits to the tavern will make you an easy target for an enemy assassin. You should have told me first.
Secret? What makes you think there's a secret?
There are no secrets here, my curious friend. Our prosperity is simply the result of hard work, good fortune and the blessings of the gods.
Heard about that, did you? Yes, I went out for a pint or two. What of it?
Balgruuf, did you slip out again last night for a drink at the Bannered Mare?
Have you met Rorik? He owns these lands, and it's from him that our village gets its name. We've been friends for many years now.
Rorik fought for the Empire in the Great War. He was gravely wounded, and so was brought before me. I was a healer then, you see.
We were as close as kinsmen, and when Rorik returned home I came with him. I am happy to spend my twilight years here with my good friend.
Yes, my lord.
Nonsense. If Balgruuf falls, someone will take his place, for better or worse. In the meantime, we have a war to win. And win it we must.
Not yet, anyway, though it certainly could.
With most of the hold's fighting men committed to the war, there are fewer swords to keep the roads safe from brigands and wild beasts.
And of course, as the war drags on and supplies run low, our ample fields and stores of food will make a tempting target for desperate men.
The best we can hope for: a swift end to the conflict. The longer the war goes on, the worse it will be for all of us, I'm afraid.
Our coffers are nearly empty. I can scarcely afford to pay the guards, and we need every one of them in these troubled times.
You'll have to make do with what we have, Proventus.
And if an assassin slips past the night watch, we won't have a jarl at all. Without Balgruuf's leadership, Whiterun will descend into chaos.
Aye, that I did. I commanded a force of several dozen men, most of them levies from villages in this part of the hold.
I damn near met my end in that war. An Aldmeri soldier ran me through with his blade and left me for dead.
A healer named Jouane saved my life. He's been my closest friend ever since. I tell you, that man is a miracle worker.
My lord, since Ulfric Stormcloak's uprising, the cost of goods has doubled. I'll need more coin if I'm to provision us properly.
What would you have me do? If we do not send men to fight in the war, you'll find yourself guarding one of Ulfric's men.
Yes, that's right. Look around you. Most of the lands you see are mine.
Most of this I purchased while my comrades were fighting in the south, helping the Empire against the Aldmeri Dominion.
Back then, nothing would grow here and so the land was worthless. Now, thanks to the hard work and the gods' blessings, our farms prosper.
Purchase more supplies, then. That's your job as steward, isn't it? Why do you trouble me with these details?
I am concerned about the jarl's safety. Too many of our guards have been redeployed to fight the Stormcloaks.
My lord, our provisions are running dangerously low. Our stores of meat, wine and grain are all but depleted.
In Whiterun, mostly. Ennis handles the business arrangements, but I know that he has a few purchasers who give us a fair price.
Ennis prides himself on his shrewd business schemes. For my part, I'm far happier working with cattle and crops than with people.
That might be so, but it will never stop me from trying.
Do I detect a bit of jealousy in your tone? I would hardly blame you. What calling could be more noble than this?
I see in your eyes that you think I jest. I assure you, I don't. I am proud, and rightfully so, of the work we do here.
Working the soil with your hands, seeing your seeds take root and grow, tending a herd... there is a joy in honest labor you won't find elsewhere.
Dammit, woman, I'm the Jarl of Whiterun! I won't apologize for talking to my people. You can't protect me every moment of the day.
Wha- Your shoes? We get robbed by bandits, my clothes torn to shreds by some giant cat, our bodyguard eaten by a dragon...
I hate it here. Those were my favorite shoes, ruined, and for what? We never even made it to the wedding.
If there's one thing I hate more than a mean-spirited drunk, it's one who can't settle their debts.
Ugh. It's talking to me.
I hate this place. I hate the trees. I hate the rocks. And I hate the snow.
Clearly you have no idea who I am you or you wouldn't dare approach me.
Just get away from me. We've got nothing left to steal.
I bet the wedding was amazing... and we missed the whole thing.
Leave me alone. I just want to find my way out of this accursed province.
When we get back to Cyrodiil, I swear I will hire an army of battlemages to come up here and set fire to the whole damned province.
All right... All right... Fine. I thought we were friends, but you're a real jerk, you know that?
Are you kidding me? I fought off all those bandits. I'm not afraid of you.
And you're worried about your shoes?
J'zargo awaits word of how well his scrolls work.
J'zargo sees what you are up to. Very well.
It is difficult to admit, but J'zargo could still use some help.
Can you... can you possibly help J'zargo?
Hey, Razelan, remember when you told me I'd never amount to anything?
Well, I don't think you should say things like that about the ambassador!
An old woman can get away with almost anything.
I'm sorry, Ambassador. I didn't mean to cause such a commotion. There's no need to have him thrown out on my account.
Yes, that's probably for the best. I really shouldn't risk getting on Elenwen's bad side.
I don't think so. Sorry. I would love to help you, but Elenwen can be very vindictive. I wouldn't want to get on her bad side.
I know you're drunk as usual, but that's no excuse for insulting our hostess.
I don't think anyone deserves that kind of abuse! I've known plenty of elves who were perfectly decent people.
Oh? Something interesting, I hope?
That is absolutely untrue! Uh, that is, yes, of course. What... I don't understand why everyone... never mind.
Well, nothing would please me more. I'll keep their eyes away while you do what you came here for.
I don't even want to know, do I? But for you... all right. I'll do it.
Ha ha! That's funny. Ya' think you can take me on? Try it anytime. I've been itching for a fight.
The wizards called it the Quill of Gemination and demanded a tremendous amount of gold to have it made.
If you can locate the quill and return it to me, something Vald was too stupid to do himself, I'll consider the debt satisfied.
A few years back, I commissioned a unique quill from a good friend of mine in Winterhold.
Vald was tasked with getting it here safely and it ended up at the bottom of Lake Honrich.
Hang on Mercer! I'm coming to help!
